region_groups.name: Great Plains
region_groups.description: The northern and central Great Plains—wind-swept, lower rainfall, and shorter outdoor growing seasons outside irrigated pockets.

regions.name: North Dakota
regions.description: North Dakota’s short frost-free window favors spring wheat, barley, beans, and sugar beets—local produce peaks sharply in late summer.

regions.name: South Dakota
regions.description: South Dakota blends prairie grain production with Black Hills orchards and summer sweet corn; season starts later than Corn Belt neighbors.

regions.name: Nebraska
regions.description: Nebraska’s Sandhills and Platte valley support corn, beef, and irrigated crops—garden harvest concentrates in mid-to-late summer.

regions.name: Kansas
regions.description: Kansas is wheat and grain sorghum country with surprising melon and sweet corn pockets; hot summers end dryland seasons quickly.
